executiveSummary:
With ALD Engineering & Construction LLC as main participant (SME) and ZNU Laboratory of Parallel and Distributed Computing as supporting participant, the consortium resolves the critical legal entity barrier identified in the previous assessment. ALD is a legitimate LLC with 219 employees, qualifying as an SME under EU definitions. The company possesses a unique 6-year proprietary procurement dataset and operates a SaaS platform for construction documentation — providing both the training data and the business case required by the grant.

The CosTERRA project (domain-specific generative AI model for automated generation of construction cost estimation documents) represents a genuine generative AI use case that aligns with FFplus Call-2-Type-2 objectives. The justification for custom model development is strong: Ukrainian construction norms (DBN, DSTU) are not represented in existing LLMs, and the structured nature of cost estimation documents requires specialized generation capabilities.

ZNU's Laboratory provides essential AI/ML and HPC expertise through Dr. Halyna Shylo's team, complementing ALD's domain knowledge and data assets. The consortium structure is well-balanced with clear role separation.

Remaining risks: (1) PIC code registration is URGENT — must be completed before Feb 25 deadline, (2) No previous EU grant experience, (3) Generative AI expertise needs to be clearly demonstrated, (4) HPC resource justification needs specificity.

breakdown.explanation: Ukraine is officially associated with Digital Europe Programme since September 2022. Both ALD and ZNU are eligible.
ALD Engineering & Construction is a registered LLC — an independent legal entity qualifying as SME. 219 employees is well under 250 threshold. Only gap: PIC code not yet registered (URGENT).
ZNU Laboratory has strong HPC expertise (parallel and distributed computing specialization). No direct experience with EuroHPC systems, but solid foundation for utilizing pre-exascale resources.
ZNU has general AI/ML expertise but limited specific experience with LLM training/fine-tuning. The consortium will need to demonstrate credible capability in transformer architecture fine-tuning.
Combined: ALD (219 employees, software development for ERP/SaaS) + ZNU Lab (4 researchers, 1 PhD, 1 Professor). Good complementarity.
Neither partner has EU grant experience. ZNU has national grant experience (3.59M UAH).
ALD manages large-scale structured data in production ERP system (6 years). FAIR principles and EU-specific DMP will need attention.
STRONG: ALD is a commercial company with existing SaaS business model. Clear path to commercialization. This is a major strength.
Well-structured consortium: ALD (domain, data, business) + ZNU Lab (AI/ML, HPC, research). Clear complementarity.
ALD has 6 years of proprietary procurement data. Dataset is available and under ALD's control. Needs conversion to training format.

strengths.description: ALD Engineering & Construction is a registered LLC with 219 employees, fully qualifying as SME under EU definitions.
6 years of production procurement data including cost estimation documents, material databases, pricing history, and project records.
ALD operates a commercial SaaS platform for construction documentation. Direct integration path for AI model. Clear revenue model, existing customers.
Automated generation of cost estimation documents is a legitimate generative AI application. Ukrainian construction norms are absent from existing LLMs.
ALD (domain expertise, data, business) + ZNU Lab (AI/ML, HPC, research) — clear role separation with non-overlapping expertise.
Ukraine is associated with Digital Europe Programme since September 2022. Both consortium members are eligible.

weaknesses.description: ALD must register for a PIC code on the EU Participant Portal before the February 25, 2026 deadline.
weaknesses.mitigation: Initiate registration IMMEDIATELY. Prepare all required documents.

weaknesses.description: Neither partner has specific published experience in LLM fine-tuning or generative model training.
weaknesses.mitigation: Frame as innovation study building generative AI capability. Emphasize ML foundations and software engineering capacity.

weaknesses.description: Neither partner has managed EU-funded projects.
weaknesses.mitigation: Allocate administrative capacity. Study FFplus guidelines. Consider consultant with EU grant experience.

weaknesses.description: Must justify computational requirements exceeding EuroHPC playground access with specific estimates.
weaknesses.mitigation: Provide detailed GPU-hour estimates, model size calculations, training duration projections.

gaps.description: PIC code registration required for ALD before submission deadline.
gaps.recommendation: Register immediately on EU Participant Portal.
Define specific architecture: base model, parameter count, fine-tuning strategy, custom components.

gaps.description: Data Management Plan aligned with FAIR principles not yet developed.
Trustworthy AI self-assessment not yet completed.
gaps.recommendation: Identify risks specific to construction document generation and develop mitigations.

risks.description: PIC registration may not complete before deadline. Call may close at 250 proposals.
risks.mitigation: Start registration immediately. Prepare proposal in parallel.

risks.description: Evaluators may score Excellence low if generative AI expertise is not convincingly demonstrated.
risks.mitigation: Describe technical approach with specific architectures, methodologies, and evaluation frameworks.

risks.description: Competing against established European AI startups with proven track records.
risks.mitigation: Differentiate through unique use case, proprietary dataset, and clear business model.

requiredActions.action: Register ALD for PIC code on EU Participant Portal immediately
Define specific model architecture and training parameters
Develop Data Management Plan following FAIR principles

strategicRecommendations: STRONG POSITION: The consortium has a viable path with ALD as SME main participant. Proprietary data + commercial business model + academic AI/HPC expertise is competitive.
DIFFERENTIATION: Construction cost estimation use case is niche enough to stand out against generic AI proposals.
URGENT: PIC code registration must happen immediately. Single biggest risk.
PRESENT CONSORTIUM COMPLEMENTARITY CLEARLY: ALD brings business, data, market; ZNU brings AI, HPC, research.
AIM FOR EARLIEST POSSIBLE SUBMISSION: Deadline Feb 25, call may close at 250 proposals.
